results.description:
DESCRIPTION:THIS PROJECT WILL ESTABLISH METHODS WITHIN A RISK-BASED DECISION FRAMEWORK FOR USING NEW APPROACH METHODOLOGIES (NAMS) BY INTEGRATING NEW BIOLOGICAL (I.E. TISSUE CHIPS), ANALYTICAL, AND COMPUTATIONAL MODELING METHODS FOR DATA-DRIVEN IN VITRO - IN VIVO EXTRAPOLATIONS (IVIVE). THE DATA-DRIVEN TOXICOKINETIC MODELING DEVELOPED WILL BE TESTED WITH A WIDE RANGE OF CHEMICAL COMPOUNDS AND CHEMICAL MIXTURES DEVISED TO MODEL REALISTIC REAL-WORLD EXPOSURES.ACTIVITIES:THIS RESEARCH AIMS TO INTEGRATE NEWLY VALIDATED BIOLOGICAL ASSAYS (MICROPHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEMS OF THE LIVER, KIDNEY AND GUT) WITH DEMONSTRATED ANALYTICAL ION MOBILITY SPECTROMETRY-MASS SPECTROMETRY (IMS-MS) METHOD AND TO COUPLE THEM WITH A RECENTLY DEVELOPED DATABASE OF THOUSANDS OF ENVIRONMENTAL CHEMICALS. CONSISTENT WITH THE STATUTORY ACTIVITIES ABOVE, THIS PROJECT WILL ALLOW FOR REFINED DATA-DRIVEN IVIVE (IN VITRO-TO-IN VIVO) EXTRAPOLATIONS, FURTHER IMPROVE RISK-BASED DECISIONS ON ENVIRONMENTAL CHEMICALS AND MIXTURES; AND ALLOW FOR MORE ROBUST AND STANDARDIZED NEW APPROACH METHODOLOGIES (NAMS). THIS PROJECT RELATES TO TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA) SECTION (A) CONDUCT RESEARCH, DEVELOPMENT, AND MONITORING AS NECESSARY.

SUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES:THIS GRANT PROVIDES FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TO THE TEXAS AANDM UNIVERSITY TO IMPLEMENT ITS OWN PROJECT TO FURTHER DEVELOP IMPROVED HIGH-THROUGHPUT SCREENING METHODS. IN PARTICULAR, THE INTEGRATED SET OF NOVEL BIOLOGICAL ASSAYS (I.E., TISSUE CHIPS), ANALYTICAL TECHNIQUES (I.E., IMS-MS) AND COMPUTATIONAL METHODS THAT RESULT WILL BE USED TO ENABLE HIGH-FIDELITY, DATA-DRIVEN TOXICOKINETIC MODELS FOR USE IN PREDICTIVE CHEMICAL SCREENS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE THE GENERAL PUBLIC WHO WILL BENEFIT FROM IMPROVED CHEMICAL SAFETY DECISIONS AND SCIENTISTS AND RISK ASSESSORS WHO WILL HAVE ACCESS TO BETTER, FASTER, AND MORE INFORMATIVE TESTING OF CHEMICAL SUBSTANCES.
